threat_model: A generic continuation input could be mistaken for signed authorization if the response witness permits approval flags, runtime authorization gate satisfaction, or runtime authority fields. The mitigation is a schema-backed local-only rejection witness with GovernanceBlocked, RejectedNoEffect, hard false authority fields, and an explicit remaining gate for a signed runtime authorization approval witness.

required_checks.check: authority boundary check proves the rejection witness keeps runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success claims, terminal closure, and runtime execution false
approval-drift check proves generic continuation cannot set signed approval, runtime authorization gate satisfaction, or runtime activation allowance
witness integrity check proves the checked-in fixture matches the deterministic produced witness and validates against the schema
response-boundary check proves observed input remains generic continuation, explicit signed approval remains required, and runtime activation is not performed

findings.mitigation: The schema and validator hard-deny operator_approval_collected, signed_approval_present, runtime_authorization_gate_satisfied, runtime_activation_allowed, runtime_promotion_authorized, execution_allowed, dispatch_allowed, runtime_replanning_enabled, success_claim_allowed, terminal_closure, and runtime execution fields.
The rejection witness binds the source runtime authorization request id and hash and rejects drift in submitted, response-required, response-collected, and authorization-gate fields.

mitigations: keep the generic continuation rejection witness local, deterministic, and read-only
hard-deny all runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success, terminal closure, activation, and runtime execution fields in the schema
bind the rejection witness to the deterministic runtime authorization request id and hash
record explicit signed approval as the remaining authorization gate before activation
